rationale: Round-2 APPROVE holds at 07eb467e after the R6 bead-unique-doc reshuffle. I re-walked each bead's source file set against the four OWNERSHIP.yaml globs and re-mapped its domain-doc targets: no source-touched domain was dropped from any bead. B1 touches source in ALL FOUR domains (workflow: hook/next/doctor/validate/panel/layout/instruct + cmd/** + plugins/mindspec/**; execution: gitutil/harness; core: recording; context-system: contextpack) and its key_file_paths carry a doc under all four dirs (workflow/execution/core/context-system architecture.md). B2 source is internal/setup/** only (safeio is called, not edited) -> workflow/overview.md. B3 source is internal/complete (workflow) + internal/phase (core) -> workflow/interfaces.md + core/overview.md. B4 source is cmd/** (workflow); AGENTS.md is a rootOperatorDoc that never counts -> workflow/runbook.md. Every bead therefore still supplies >=1 changed doc under .mindspec/domains/<domain>/ for every domain its non-test source touches, clearing checkInternalPackages with no --allow-doc-skew. The eight doc targets are all distinct (zero shared key_file_paths; confirmed by parsing the work_chunks frontmatter) and all exist on disk (appends). Plan validator exits 0 with only the known intentional scope-redundancy WARN. docsync gate logic is unchanged between rounds, so my round-2 mechanics analysis still stands. No gate-blocker.

findings.title: Reshuffle to bead-unique doc targets preserved full domain coverage per bead — CONFIRMED
findings.detail: Re-derived source-touched domains from OWNERSHIP.yaml at 07eb467e and matched them to each work_chunk's key_file_paths doc entries. B1: {workflow, execution, core, context-system} -> {workflow/architecture.md, execution/architecture.md, core/architecture.md, context-system/architecture.md} (ALL FOUR present, per BRIEF requirement). B2: {workflow} -> workflow/overview.md (safeio not in file set). B3: {workflow, core} -> workflow/interfaces.md + core/overview.md. B4: {workflow} -> workflow/runbook.md. No domain a bead edits source in lacks a doc touch; the reshuffle moved paths without dropping coverage.

findings.title: Eight doc targets are bead-unique and all exist on disk — CONFIRMED
findings.detail: Parsed the work_chunks frontmatter only (awk from work_chunks: to the closing ---): the eight domain-doc paths each appear exactly once, so no doc path is shared by two beads (R6's ask). All eight files exist on disk, so each bead's touch is an append to an existing doc — irrelevant to the gate, which checks membership in the changed set, but confirms the Steps are actionable.

findings.title: plan-approve validator clean at 07eb467e (exit 0) — CONFIRMED
findings.detail: `mindspec validate plan 107-cleanup-deadcode-dry-wave1` exits 0 with a single WARN: decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.04 below threshold 0.15. Validator shape/frontmatter parse intact; four work_chunks, all depends_on: [], six ADR citations covering all four impacted domains.

findings.title: Advisory: scope-redundancy R fell 0.08 -> 0.04 as a direct consequence of the bead-unique-doc cut
findings.detail: Because R6 removed all shared doc paths across beads, the measured cross-bead shared context dropped further below threshold. This is the intended ownership-aligned decomposition (every deletion rides the bead that owns the file; no shared source or doc), already acknowledged by prior rounds and the validator as a non-blocking WARN. Advisory only, not a gate-blocker — no change required.
